description:
In lower_prim's BinOp arm, the typed float/int fast-path check appears twice in nearly
identical form — once for arithmetic (Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Mod) and once for comparison
(Lt/Le/Gt/Ge). Each follows the same structure:

  if both args are typed float -> early return LowerOut::RawF64(float_op(af, bf))
  if both args are typed int   -> early return LowerOut::RawI64(int_op(ai, bi))
  // else fall through to emit_dispatch_binop(...)

ADD a helper (private, before lower_prim):

  fn try_typed_binop_fast_path(
      fn_types: &crate::ir_typer::FnTypes,
      a: &crate::fz_ir::Var,
      b_var: &crate::fz_ir::Var,
      b: &mut FunctionBuilder<'_>,
      var_env: &HashMap<u32, VarBinding>,
      float_op: impl FnOnce(&mut FunctionBuilder<'_>, ir::Value, ir::Value) -> ir::Value,
      int_op: impl FnOnce(&mut FunctionBuilder<'_>, ir::Value, ir::Value) -> ir::Value,
  ) -> Option<LowerOut>

Returns Some(LowerOut::RawF64/RawI64) if both args have a narrow typed Descr matching the
fast path; returns None to signal fall-through to emit_dispatch_binop.

UPDATE the arithmetic arm and comparison arm in BinOp to call try_typed_binop_fast_path,
replacing the ~8 lines of inline float/int type checking in each arm.

The Eq/Neq arm has a different structure (disjoint-kind fold, different slow path) and
should NOT be changed by this ticket.

cargo build must produce zero warnings. All tests must pass.
title: fz-cg3.4 consolidate typed fast-path dispatch in BinOp arm
